meta.test_video: b65 — Guy Monroe AI Marketing Plan

pattern.name: Blackboard Workflow Pattern
pattern.tagline: Stateful, durable, stigmergic agent workflows in Claude Code
pattern.elevator: AWB replacement that runs inside Claude Code. Conductor (orchestrator role) delegates each step to isolated subagents coordinating through a durable JSONL store. Solves the Oscar context-poisoning problem by giving each worker a clean context and writing payloads directly to disk — orchestrator only sees acks.

core_components.name: workflow.json
core_components.role: spec — DAG of steps, each declaring inputs and outputs

core_components.name: Conductor
core_components.role: orchestrator role (the Claude Code session you talk to) — never accumulates payloads

core_components.name: Subagent
core_components.role: isolated worker — clean context, write-store-directly contract

core_components.name: Store
core_components.role: EAV append-only JSONL — single source of truth, projected on read

worker_contracts.name: write-store-directly
worker_contracts.use: Default. Worker writes the store, returns 1-line ack. Max isolation.

worker_contracts.name: conductor-writes
worker_contracts.use: Conductor itself writes (no subagent). For checkpoint/seed/manifest steps.

worker_contracts.name: return-summary
worker_contracts.use: Worker returns payload to orchestrator. Used only when orchestrator must reason about it (human gate). Bounded poisoning.
worker_contracts.where_used: Reserved — not exercised in any probe yet

isolation_rules: Workers are subagents — clean context, return only one-line acks
Conductor log captures only dispatches, acks, gate transitions, timing — never payloads
Transcript stays on disk; orchestrator holds only the path string
Image bytes never leave disk; store records carry localPath only
Critique text lives in store, not log; log records only byte length

probes.title: Bulk Content Analysis
probes.tested: Parallel fan-out — 12 subagents extract distinct content-analysis fields concurrently
probes.tagline: Can the conductor stay clean under 12-way parallel writes?
probes.key_insight: Distinct-key fan-out works. Each worker has clean context, writes its own EAV record, returns ack. Orchestrator log contains only 12 dispatches + 12 acks. Zero transcript fragments leaked.
probes.prompts_used: 12 .hbs files lifted verbatim from AWB Gen 3 content-analysis

probes.title: Title Generation + Scripted Refinement
probes.tested: Sequential refinement loop with fixture-based critique + outputKeyMap key rename
probes.tagline: Does the refinement loop preserve attempt history while keeping orchestrator clean?
probes.step_type: transform → checkpoint (conductor-writes from fixture) → transform with outputKeyMap
probes.key_insight: Stateless re-dispatch refinement works. Fresh subagent reads prior attempt + critique from store, produces refined output. outputKeyMap allows one step to write two distinct store keys. Ordering GT1 → CL → GT2 → ST verified.

probes.title: Real Human-Gated Refinement
probes.tested: Live human-in-the-loop critique via stdin — both accept-path and refine-path branches
probes.tagline: Can the orchestrator host an interactive conversation without polluting the store-fed workers?
probes.step_type: transform → checkpoint (humanGate human-stdin) → transform with outputKeyMap
probes.key_insight: Both gate paths exercised in same run. Accept-path (typed 'ok') skipped refine and wrote selectedTitles with meta.skippedRefinement:true. Refine-path (real critique) wrote titleCritiqueLog with meta.source:human-stdin, dispatched fresh refine worker. Append-only store preserved both as audit history.
probes.footnote: Probe surfaced a /goal-vs-human-gate friction (stop hook nudges against paused-gate state). Resolved architecturally: /goal is a build-time tool, conductor is the runtime — orthogonal layers.

probes.title: kie.ai Thumbnails with Title Pairing
probes.tested: Async external API + binary artifacts + multi-stage flow + title-thumbnail pairing invariant
probes.tagline: Does the isolation hold for binary outputs from a long-running external API?
probes.step_type: transform → 6× transform parallel (kie.ai) → checkpoint (fixture) → 3× transform parallel (kie.ai 2K) → action
probes.ac_notes: AC22 cost $0.36 over $0.20 budget — flux-schnell substitution. AC21 baseline diff included probe 3's uncommitted skill edits.
probes.key_insight: Pattern works for external APIs and binary outputs. Workers curl -o JPGs to disk, store record carries localPath only. Title pairing invariant held end-to-end — every final's title_ref matches its source exploration's title_ref. Zero image bytes in store, zero kie.ai response bodies in conductor log.
known_issues: `flux-schnell` is not on kie.ai — workers fell back to flux-2/pro-text-to-image
First-pass exploration records (idx 2, 5) claim model:flux-schnell but used different models
nano-banana-2 returned square (1:1) for 2 of 3 finals — treats aspect_ratio as a hint, prompt body cues override

skills.name: conductor
skills.role: Documents the orchestrator role — §5 execution loop, hard isolation rules, subagent task templates, log line formats, human gate semantics, outputKeyMap, resumability

skills.name: store
skills.role: Bash+jq EAV operations — remember (append), recall (last-write-wins), project (fold to view.json)

skills.name: image-gen
skills.role: kie.ai async wrapper — POST createTask, poll recordInfo, curl download, write store record with localPath
skills.spec_drift_note: References flux-schnell which is not a kie.ai model; needs update to flux-2/pro-text-to-image or equivalent

open_loops.issue: flux-schnell is a phantom model — referenced in image-gen skill + workflow-thumbnails.json but kie.ai rejects it
open_loops.fix: Update both files to flux-2/pro-text-to-image (verified working)

open_loops.issue: First-pass exploration records carry inaccurate model field
open_loops.fix: Append corrected EAV records (last-write-wins preserves honesty)

open_loops.issue: nano-banana-2 aspect-ratio drift on finals 0 and 2
open_loops.fix: Add explicit horizontal prompt language to generate-design-brief.hbs
Document orthogonal-layers rule explicitly in conductor/SKILL.md

open_loops.issue: runs/b65-2-5/ naming inconsistent with ordinal convention
open_loops.fix: Rename to runs/b65-humangate/ or similar (cosmetic only)

what_each_probe_enabled.now_possible: Run N parallel content-analysis or extraction tasks against any transcript-like input
Iterative refinement with fixture-driven critique; one step writing multiple distinct store keys
Real human-in-the-loop workflows where the orchestrator hosts the conversation and only distilled critique moves forward
Production-shaped workflows that call external APIs, persist binary artifacts to disk, and maintain cross-stage data integrity invariants like title-thumbnail pairing
